phases.name: Foundation
phases.summary: Open consulate doors, establish legal structure, identify core partners and artists

phases.name: Programming & Funding Applications
phases.summary: Submit grant applications, file P-3 visa petitions, lock venues

phases.name: Approvals & Publicity
phases.summary: Visa approvals, public announcement, ticket launch, press

phases.name: Production Ramp
phases.summary: Production hire, marketing intensification, technical preparation

phases.name: Festival Execution
phases.summary: Artist arrivals, festival in Seattle and Bay Area, wrap and Year 2 kickoff

milestones.action: Email hoc.seattle@mea.gov.in — meeting request with concept note
Contact CG SF office to request cultural affairs meeting
Begin Fractured Atlas fiscal sponsorship application
Direct outreach to Anjani Saraf for letter of support
Draft 1-page festival concept note
Fractured Atlas approval expected
Meeting with CGI Seattle
Meeting with CGI San Francisco
Reach out to Roundglass India Center (Sital Kalantry)
Cold email 3 Indian-arts 501(c)(3) organizations
Meeting with Seattle University Office of Global Engagement
Meeting with UW South Asia Center
Meeting with Stanford or UC Berkeley South Asia center
Begin artist curation — circulate brief to advisors in India
Lock provisional festival dates
Finalize lead artist list (top 5-8 candidates)
Confirm US co-presenter partnership
Begin outreach to immigration attorney for P-3 work
Submit ICCR Travel Grant applications via CGI Seattle endorsement
Check current US Embassy PAS RFP on grants.gov
Send first 3 corporate sponsor outreach letters
Request CGI endorsement letters for grants and visas
Submit US Embassy PAS Small Grant (if RFP open)
Identify 3 expert letter writers per artist for P-3 petitions
Begin labor organization consultation (AGMA / AGVA / AFM)
Venue contracts under negotiation in both cities
Lock primary venues in Seattle and Bay Area
Submit California Arts Council grant (if open)
Submit 4Culture grant (if open)
Submit ACTA Living Cultures grant (if applicable)
Push corporate sponsors for commitments
GATE CHECK: At least 2 major funders committed
File Form I-129 P-3 visa petitions for all artists (Premium Processing)
Confirm expert letters signed and in evidence package
Press release drafted (not yet published)
USCIS I-129 decisions arriving (15 days from filing with premium)
Forward Form I-797 approval notices to artists
Artists begin DS-160 and US Consulate interview scheduling
Issue formal invitation letters on festival letterhead
Public festival announcement
Press outreach (New India Abroad, Seattle Times, KQED, etc.)
Ticket sales launch with early-bird pricing
Website and marketing materials live
Artist visa interviews in India
Activate community partners for word of mouth
Confirm artist visa stamping complete
Flight bookings for all artists
Hotel blocks reserved
GATE CHECK: All artists have visas in passport
Production team hired (stage managers, tech, hospitality)
Detailed run-of-show for each venue
Volunteer recruitment
Print program books, posters, signage
Second press cycle (feature stories, artist profiles)
GATE CHECK: Ticket sales at 50% of target
Final artist itineraries confirmed
Technical rehearsals scheduled
Final venue walk-throughs
Artists begin arriving in US
SEATTLE Day 1: Opening gala + headliner concert
SEATTLE Day 2: Lecture-demos at Seattle U + evening performance
SEATTLE Day 3: Community showcase at Seattle Center
BAY AREA Day 5: Masterclass at Stanford/UC Berkeley + evening at Zellerbach
BAY AREA Day 6: Flagship concert at CPA San Jose
BAY AREA Day 7: Closing community event
Artists return to India
Grant reporting documentation (photos, attendance, press)
Thank-you letters to sponsors, consulates, venues
Year 2 planning kickoff

criticalPath: Week 4: Fiscal sponsor confirmed + artists identified
